_comment: Golden Goose Eggs — the 3-5 outcomes that matter most right now. Defined and redefined by theme-review. Orient checks these first. A failing GGE triggers an immediate priority-0 backlog story.

eggs.title: Delivery machine is self-orienting and self-healing
eggs.rationale: The ceremonies loop must decide what to do next without human input, heal SMART failures by splitting, and close sprints cleanly regardless of completion. This is the meta-capability that accelerates everything else. Fragility elevated after monorepo restructure — ceremony scripts may reference old paths (bootstrap/, charts/, vendor/) that no longer exist at the same locations.

eggs.title: Living state docs answer 'where are we' without reading history
eggs.rationale: Every agent that touches this repo must be able to orient in under 2 minutes by reading two files. Without this, every session starts with archaeology. Rewritten each sprint by the sync ceremony. Currently fragile: agent.md shows increment 16 as active after the restructure that moved to increment 17 pending.

eggs.title: Planning pipeline always has a pending increment to execute
eggs.rationale: The plan ceremony cannot run without a pending increment in manifest.json. When all increments complete, the machine stalls silently. This egg has triggered three remediation sprints (11, 13, 15) — it is the most proven fragility in the delivery machine.

indicator.command: python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1, f'No pending increments — plan ceremony will stall. Run theme-review to create one.'; print(f'{len(pending)} pending increment(s) — OK')"

eggs.title: Zero external registry references in Helm chart templates
eggs.rationale: Sovereignty requires that no chart template ever hard-codes an external registry. Every image reference must use {{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}. IMPORTANT: indicator updated from charts/*/templates/ to platform/charts/*/templates/ after monorepo restructure — the old path pointed at an empty directory and produced a vacuous PASS, providing no protection.

indicator.command: bash -c 'result=$(grep -rn "docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io" platform/charts/*/templates/ 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$result" ] && echo "G6 PASS: no external registries in templates" || (echo "G6 FAIL:" && echo "$result" && exit 1)'

eggs.title: Contract validator enforces sovereignty invariants — test suite passes
eggs.rationale: contract/validate.py is the new machine-enforced sovereignty gate introduced in the monorepo restructure. It is the only mechanism that guarantees autarky.externalEgressBlocked=true before any cluster is provisioned. If the test suite breaks (valid.yaml rejected or invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml accepted), the sovereignty invariant is silently unenforced. G3 (governance doc file_exists) retired — the validator replaces it with actual enforcement. Fragile now because the validator is new and its test corpus is minimal.

indicator.command: bash -c 'python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 || (echo "G7 FAIL: valid.yaml rejected by validator" && exit 1); python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && (echo "G7 FAIL: invalid contract accepted — autarky invariant not enforced" && exit 1); echo "G7 PASS: contract validator enforces sovereignty invariants"'

_retired.title: Cluster contract is current, enforced, and machine-checked
_retired.retiredReason: Graduated to table stakes. The file_exists check on docs/governance/cluster-contract.md has been healthy for 10+ consecutive sprints. The monorepo restructure introduced contract/validate.py which provides actual machine enforcement — that is now tracked by G7. A governance document is not the right indicator when a real validator exists.

_retired.title: Mental model is coherent — one vocabulary, one hierarchy
_retired.retiredReason: Graduated to table stakes. Vocabulary migration (increments/phases) completed many sprints ago. The gate_passing check always passes and has never triggered a remediation sprint. The word 'phases' does not appear in new code and the hard-stop rule in agent.md enforces this at the implementation level. Retiring frees a slot for fragility that is real and current.
